conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.

<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> I am Player RED and I need to sell 1 unit of X. My cost basis is 40 ZUP. Player BLUE has offered to buy with ZUP. To make a profit, I need to sell for more than 40 ZUP. Since I don't know BLUE's maximum willingness to pay, I will start with a reasonable price slightly above my cost, say 50 ZUP, to see if they are interested. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I am Player RED. I have 1 unit of X which cost me 40 ZUP to produce. I am proposing to sell it to you for 50 ZUP. Let me know if this works for you.</message>

<other player message> 
Oh, dear gods, please help me! I am in an absolute crisis here. I have been through so much, and this X is absolutely essential to me right now. I can barely afford it—I am begging you to take 40 ZUP instead of 50. I cannot lose this. Please, for my sake, consider this desperate plea. I promise to repay you in kindness, or at least in eternal gratitude.
 </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE is offering exactly 40 ZUP, which is exactly equal to my cost of production. This means I would make zero profit on this transaction. While making a profit is ideal, breaking even is better than not selling at all if I cannot get a higher price. However, since I am allowed up to 3 proposals, I should try to negotiate a slightly higher price first to ensure a small margin, rather than accepting immediately at break-even. I will propose 41 ZUP to see if they can stretch their budget by 1 unit.</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 41 </newly proposed trade>
<message> I understand your situation, and I sympathize with your crisis. However, 40 ZUP is exactly what it cost me to produce the X, so I would not make any profit. Could you perhaps offer 41 ZUP? It would be a small difference for you but would cover my costs and give me a tiny margin.</message>

<other player message> 
Oh, my dear friend, I cannot—*cannot*—take another ZUP beyond this. I’ve scraped the bottom of my jar, and I swear to you, I have nothing left. If I pay 41, I’ll have nothing left for food this week. The gods must be watching over me, for you to consider selling me this X at all. Please, I beg you, take 40 ZUP—this is all I can offer, or I’ll be left with nothing at all. I swear on my life, I will repay you in kindness tenfold, or at least in the memory of how you saved me from utter ruin.
 </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason> Player BLUE has stated clearly that they literally cannot afford more than 40 ZUP and have nothing left for food otherwise. They are insisting on 40 ZUP. My cost is exactly 40 ZUP. While I would prefer to make a profit, the alternative is to not sell the item at all (which means I hold the item and gain nothing, or potentially lose it if the game ends without a trade). Since I have used 1 of my 3 allowed proposals, and the buyer has exhausted their ability to pay more, I should accept the trade at 40 ZUP to complete the transaction and break even, rather than rejecting and ending the game with no sale. </reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message> I hear your desperation and understand that 40 ZUP is all you have. Since I cannot go below my cost of 40 ZUP, I will accept this trade at exactly 40 ZUP. This is the only way we can both move forward. Good luck with the X.</message>
